title: Tapping for Who Am I Now: Early Motherhood
subtitle: A tapping practice for the identity shift that comes with becoming a mother
excerpt: A tapping practice for the identity disorientation of early motherhood. The script holds both realities: loving the child and missing the self that existed before. For use when the becoming feels more like a loss than a gain.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The EFT tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, developed in the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use in the early months of motherhood when the identity shift is most disorienting. Good when loving the child and missing yourself are both true at once and feel difficult to hold together.

content.text: A tapping practice for the identity disorientation of early motherhood. The script holds both things that are true: the love for the child, and the missing of the self that existed before. Neither cancels the other out.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though I have lost track of who I am since the baby arrived, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I love this child and also miss myself, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I do not know how to be both a mother and the person I was, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** Who am I now?
**Side of eye:** I became a mother and something shifted.
**Under eye:** The person I was before is still in here.
**Under nose:** But she is different now and I am not sure who she is.
**Chin:** I love this child.
**Collarbone:** And I also miss myself.
**Under arm:** No one told me both of those could be true at the same time.
**Top of head:** I am allowed to be in the middle of this.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** Becoming a mother is an identity rewrite.
**Side of eye:** It is not an erasure.
**Under eye:** The person I was is still in here.
**Under nose:** She is becoming someone new.
**Chin:** I do not have to have the new identity figured out yet.
**Collarbone:** I am in the middle of a becoming.
**Under arm:** That is not a problem to solve. It is a process to be in.
**Top of head:** I am allowed to not know who I am yet.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. EFT tapping was developed by Gary Craig in the 1990s.
